description: Exam 01 Final — Verbal Tenses Question Bank v2 (1st Bimester) — NO 3rd person singular, NO going to
notes: All questions use I / you / we / they subjects. No 3rd person singular. No 'going to' future — confirmed absent from the Verbal Tenses SRS deck reviewed by students. Simple Future tested exclusively with 'will'. Each completion question includes a time marker or structural anchor that makes exactly one option grammatically correct. No overlap with prep_exam_bank.json or exam_01_final_bank.json.

questions.question_text: They _____ in New York every summer.
questions.rationale: Tests Simple Present affirmative for habitual/repeated facts. 'Every summer' is a canonical Simple Present frequency marker, ruling out past and future tenses.

distractor_rationale.a: lived — Simple Past; 'every summer' signals a recurring fact, not a completed past event
distractor_rationale.c: are living — Present Continuous; used for temporary situations happening now, not recurring facts
distractor_rationale.d: will live — Simple Future; 'every summer' is habitual, not a one-off future event

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: They _____ like coffee.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'don't' auxiliary for Simple Present negative. All four options are auxiliaries from different tenses or persons; only 'don't' matches 'they' in Simple Present.

distractor_rationale.a: doesn't — 3rd person singular auxiliary (he/she/it), wrong for 'they'
distractor_rationale.b: isn't — auxiliary for Present Continuous negative, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.c: didn't — Simple Past negative auxiliary, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: _____ you enjoy swimming?
questions.rationale: Tests the 'Do' auxiliary for Simple Present questions with subject 'you'. The base verb 'enjoy' (not 'enjoying' or 'enjoyed') is the structural anchor.

distractor_rationale.a: Are — auxiliary for Present Continuous; would need 'Are you enjoying'
distractor_rationale.c: Does — 3rd person singular auxiliary, wrong for 'you'
distractor_rationale.d: Did — Simple Past auxiliary; would signal a completed past action

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.card_source: They bought a new car yesterday.
questions.question_text: They _____ a new car yesterday.
questions.rationale: Tests the irregular Simple Past form 'bought'. 'Yesterday' is a specific past time marker that forces Simple Past and rules out all other tenses.

distractor_rationale.a: buy — Simple Present base form; contradicts past time marker 'yesterday'
distractor_rationale.b: are buying — Present Continuous; 'yesterday' is a past marker, not ongoing-now
distractor_rationale.d: have bought — Present Perfect; incompatible with specific time marker 'yesterday'

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: I watched a movie last night.
questions.question_text: "I watched a movie last night." — Which tense is used in this sentence?
questions.rationale: Tests recognition of the Simple Past (-ed form) in context. 'Last night' is a specific past time marker that confirms Simple Past.

distractor_rationale.a: Simple Present: 'I watch a movie' — base form, no time marker
distractor_rationale.b: Present Continuous: 'I am watching a movie' — am + -ing structure
distractor_rationale.d: Present Perfect: 'I have watched a movie' — cannot be used with specific past marker 'last night'

questions.instruction_pt: Qual tempo verbal é usado nesta frase?

questions.card_source: I am learning to play the guitar.
questions.question_text: I _____ to play the guitar right now.
questions.rationale: Tests the am + verb-ing structure for Present Continuous. 'Right now' is the canonical present-moment marker, forcing the continuous aspect.

distractor_rationale.a: learn — Simple Present; used for habits/facts ('I learn guitar every day'), not actions in progress at this moment
distractor_rationale.c: have learned — Present Perfect; implies accumulated experience, not an action in progress right now
distractor_rationale.d: learned — Simple Past; requires a past time marker, not 'right now'

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.card_source: We are not (aren't) having dinner yet.
questions.question_text: We _____ having dinner yet.
questions.rationale: Tests the aren't auxiliary for Present Continuous negative. The -ing form 'having' in the stem makes the tense unambiguous — only a continuous auxiliary fits.

distractor_rationale.a: don't — Simple Present auxiliary; 'don't having' is ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.b: didn't — Simple Past auxiliary; 'didn't having' is ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.d: haven't — Present Perfect auxiliary; 'haven't having' is ungrammatical

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: "Are you listening to me?" — Which tense is this question in?
questions.rationale: Tests recognition of the Are + subject + verb-ing question structure for Present Continuous.

distractor_rationale.a: Simple Present question: 'Do you listen to me?' (Do + subject + base verb)
distractor_rationale.b: Simple Past question: 'Did you listen to me?' (Did + subject + base verb)
distractor_rationale.d: Present Perfect question: 'Have you listened to me?' (Have + subject + past participle)

questions.instruction_pt: Qual tempo verbal é usado nesta pergunta?

questions.question_text: I _____ you tomorrow morning.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'will + base verb' structure for Simple Future. 'Tomorrow morning' is a specific future time marker that forces the future tense.

distractor_rationale.a: call — base form only; without an auxiliary it does not express future
distractor_rationale.b: called — Simple Past; contradicts future marker 'tomorrow morning'
distractor_rationale.c: am calling — Present Continuous; can express near-future plans but clashes with the explicit 'tomorrow morning' making 'will call' the unambiguous choice

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.tense: Simple Present / Simple Past / Simple Future
questions.question_text: Which time expression matches the Simple Future tense (with 'will')?
questions.rationale: Tests knowledge of time adverbs as tense markers. 'Next week' signals Simple Future, contrasting with Simple Past (last night), Present Continuous (right now), and Simple Present (every morning).

distractor_rationale.a: last night — signals Simple Past (completed past event)
distractor_rationale.b: right now — signals Present Continuous (action in progress)
distractor_rationale.c: every morning — signals Simple Present (habitual action)

questions.instruction_pt: Qual expressão de tempo corresponde ao Simple Future (com 'will')?

questions.card_source: I was reading when the phone rang.
questions.question_text: We _____ when the alarm went off.
questions.rationale: Tests the were + verb-ing structure for Past Continuous. The 'when + Simple Past' clause ('the alarm went off') signals an interruption of an ongoing action, the defining context for Past Continuous.

distractor_rationale.a: slept — Simple Past; makes both actions sequential rather than one interrupting the other
distractor_rationale.b: have slept — Present Perfect; wrong time frame entirely
distractor_rationale.d: had slept — Past Perfect; implies the sleeping was completed before the alarm, contradicting the 'interrupted action' meaning

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: They _____ expecting visitors.
questions.rationale: Tests the weren't auxiliary for Past Continuous negative. The -ing form 'expecting' in the stem makes the tense unambiguous — only a continuous past auxiliary fits.

distractor_rationale.a: didn't — Simple Past auxiliary; 'didn't expecting' is ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.c: haven't — Present Perfect auxiliary; wrong tense and 'haven't expecting' is ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.d: don't — Simple Present auxiliary; 'don't expecting' is ungrammatical

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text:
Choose the correct option:

"While we _____ dinner, the power _____ out."
questions.rationale: Tests the Past Continuous / Simple Past contrast. 'While' introduces the longer ongoing background action (Past Continuous: were having); the shorter interrupting event uses Simple Past (went).

distractor_rationale.b: Reverses roles — 'had dinner' treats dinner as a brief event; 'was going out' makes the power cut the background action
distractor_rationale.c: Both Past Continuous — implies both actions were simultaneous and ongoing, losing the interruption
distractor_rationale.d: Both Simple Past — treats both as sequential events, losing the 'while' overlap

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a combinação correta de tempos verbais.

questions.question_text: We _____ the new lab equipment already.
questions.rationale: Tests the have + past participle structure for Present Perfect. 'Already' signals a recently completed action with present relevance — the canonical Present Perfect marker.

distractor_rationale.a: test — Simple Present; 'already' implies completion, not habitual action
distractor_rationale.b: tested — Simple Past; would require a specific time marker (e.g., 'yesterday'), not 'already'
distractor_rationale.d: were testing — Past Continuous; implies an interrupted ongoing past action, not completion

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: _____ they arrived yet?
questions.rationale: Tests the Present Perfect question auxiliary 'Have'. 'Yet' is a classic Present Perfect question marker, and 'arrived' is a past participle — both anchor the tense.

distractor_rationale.a: Did — Simple Past auxiliary; would require base verb 'arrive', not past participle 'arrived'
distractor_rationale.b: Were — Past Continuous auxiliary; would require 'arriving' not 'arrived'
distractor_rationale.d: Do — Simple Present auxiliary; would require base verb 'arrive'

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.question_text:
Choose the correct tense for each blank:

"We _____ the city (a) LAST SUMMER. We _____ it (b) THREE TIMES."
questions.rationale: Tests the core PP vs Simple Past distinction: 'last summer' (specific past time marker) requires Simple Past; 'three times' (unspecified accumulated experience) requires Present Perfect.

distractor_rationale.b: Reverses the rule — 'have visited last summer' is ungrammatical; Present Perfect rejects specific past time markers
distractor_rationale.c: All Simple Past — misses the Present Perfect for unspecified experience ('three times')
distractor_rationale.d: All Present Perfect — 'have visited last summer' is ungrammatical

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a combinação correta de tempos verbais para as duas frases.

questions.card_source: I will not (won't) be available tomorrow.
questions.question_text: We _____ finish the project on time.
questions.rationale: Tests the 'won't' (will not) auxiliary for Simple Future negative. All four options are negation auxiliaries from different tenses; only 'won't' expresses future negation.

distractor_rationale.a: don't — Simple Present negative auxiliary, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.b: didn't — Simple Past negative auxiliary, wrong tense
distractor_rationale.d: haven't — Present Perfect negative auxiliary, wrong tense

questions.instruction_pt: Complete a frase com a forma correta do verbo.

questions.question_text: _____ they accept our offer next week?
questions.rationale: Tests the 'Will' auxiliary for Simple Future questions. The time marker 'next week' and the base verb 'accept' anchor the answer to Simple Future.

distractor_rationale.a: Do — Simple Present auxiliary; 'next week' signals future, not habitual action
distractor_rationale.b: Did — Simple Past auxiliary; contradicts future marker 'next week'
distractor_rationale.c: Have — Present Perfect auxiliary; incompatible with future marker 'next week'

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha o auxiliar correto para completar a pergunta.

questions.question_text:
Choose the correct tense for each blank:

"We _____ the contract (a) LAST MONDAY. We _____ the results (b) NEXT FRIDAY."
questions.rationale: Tests the Simple Past vs Simple Future contrast using explicit time markers: 'last Monday' forces Simple Past ('signed'); 'next Friday' forces Simple Future ('will announce').

distractor_rationale.b: Reverses the markers — 'will sign last Monday' and 'announced next Friday' are both ungrammatical
distractor_rationale.c: All Simple Past — 'announced next Friday' is ungrammatical with a future marker
distractor_rationale.d: All Simple Future — 'will sign last Monday' is ungrammatical with a past marker

questions.instruction_pt: Escolha a combinação correta de tempos verbais para as duas frases.

questions.question_text:
Read the passage:

"We usually meet on Fridays. Last Friday, we discussed the new rules. Right now, we are writing the final report. We have already sent the first draft."

Which sentence uses the Present Continuous?

options.text: We are writing the final report.
We have already sent the first draft.

questions.rationale: Tests tense recognition across four tenses in a single passage: Simple Present ('usually'), Simple Past ('Last Friday'), Present Continuous ('Right now' + are + -ing), Present Perfect ('already' + have + past participle).
questions.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e identifique qual frase usa o Present Continuous.
